text.title: product-development-trends-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-development-trends-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Accepting trend assertions without a 2025+ URL, resulting in 2023-era practices presented as 2026 trends.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM training data is structurally behind real markets. "OKRs revival" and "design sprints" were popular in 2023 and will confidently appear as 2026 trends unless a hard recency gate is enforced.

failure-mode.description: Over-indexing on US and San Francisco Bay Area sources without requiring EU or APAC primary sources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Most LLM training data and most tech media is US-centric. A trend that is maturing in SF may be early or absent in EU/APAC markets. Require at least one EU and one APAC source per trend axis.

failure-mode.description: Accepting "cross-functional teams" or "rapid pivots, quarterly cycles" as findings without a concrete artifact (org chart, job posting, public retro, case study).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These are popular LLM completions that recombine from training data regardless of evidence. Require a named team or published case study; reject cadence claims without a specific organisation and source.

failure-mode.description: Letting three citations to the same secondary article inflate confidence for a claim.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: HBR, McKinsey blog, and Forbes often cite each other. Three links to the same underlying source count as one evidence point, not three.

failure-mode.description: Using raw WebSearch results ranked by SEO rather than by publication date.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SEO-optimised blog spam ranks above primary research. Sort by published date in the scraper and down-rank domains on a vendor-blog denylist before summarising.

failure-mode.description: Conflating product-development-trends-2026 with product-development-trends (the non-year-pinned sibling methodology).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two subagents running simultaneously on overlapping prompts produce two reports that a merge agent will silently resolve by choosing one, discarding the other.
